debt.malformed.object.missing_obj_info.summary: These ObjectPool child storages are physically preserved, but they cannot be promoted to typed embedded-object nodes because the mandatory descriptor stream is absent.
specification: [MS-DOC] 2.1.4.1 states that every storage within ObjectPool contains a \003ObjInfo stream holding an ODT descriptor.
upstream: LibreOffice ww8par4.cxx and msdffimp.cxx look up \003ObjInfo when importing Word embedded objects, while wrtww8gr.cxx creates the four-byte descriptor stream when it is absent during export.

debt.malformed.table.atrdextra.summary: The FIB declares an AtrdExtra range beyond the bounded Table stream; compatible mode retains the declaration without inventing missing comment-extension records.
specification: [MS-DOC] 2.9.5 defines AtrdExtra as an array parallel to PlcfandRef, and FibRgFcLcb2000 defines fcAtrdExtra/lcbAtrdExtra as its bounded Table-stream range.
upstream: LibreOffice ww8scan.cxx accepts AtrdExtra only after both seeking to fcAtrdExtra and proving that the remaining Table stream contains lcbAtrdExtra bytes; otherwise it clears the length.
corpus_examples: LibreOffice/sw/qa/core/data/ww8/pass/CVE-2007-1347-1.doc (declared AtrdExtra is outside the stream; zero recoverable bytes)

debt.malformed.table.plcfwkb.summary: The declared PlcfWKB payload is not a valid PLC of fixed twelve-byte WKB records, so its bytes are retained only as a known malformed table.
specification: [MS-DOC] 2.9.281 defines PlcfWKB as a PLC whose data elements are WKB structures of exactly 12 bytes each.
upstream: LibreOffice ww8scan.cxx constructs its PlcfWKB reader with a fixed 12-byte data-element size, and ww8struc.hxx models the corresponding WW8_WKB record.
corpus_examples: LibreOffice/sw/qa/core/data/ww8/pass/CVE-2007-1347-1.doc (66-byte payload cannot satisfy PlcfWKB PLC framing)

debt.specification_opaque.deprecated_num.summary: The deprecated numbering field cache has no normative payload layout to expose as a Rust node; its bounded bytes remain explicit and preserved.
specification: [MS-DOC] 2.5.6 says the cache at fcPlcfBteLvc SHOULD NOT be emitted, SHOULD be ignored, and lcbPlcfBteLvc SHOULD be zero; product notes say Word 2007 and later write zero.
upstream: Apache POI names FIB field 86 as PLCFBTELVC but deliberately omits it from FileInformationBlock's typed known-field set, causing the generic unhandled-data preservation path to own it.
corpus_examples: Audited Apache POI and LibreOffice DOC corpus (114 bounded legacy cache ranges, 1,952 bytes total)

debt.unknown_extension.table.msoenvelope.summary: The payload occupies the MsoEnvelope FIB range but declares version 454682, outside the version-dependent layouts documented for versions 6 and 8; its 554 bytes are preserved without guessing an encoding.
specification: [MS-OSHARED] 2.3.8.2 defines MsoEnvelope and specifies its strings and optional fields only for Ver 6 and Ver 8.
upstream: Apache POI HWPF has no typed MsoEnvelope decoder and routes unrecognized FIB-referenced data through its UnhandledDataStructure preservation mechanism.

debt.compatibility.type_0x0000.summary: Producer padding and legacy envelopes use recType 0x0000; preserve them, but do not treat them as a new MS-PPT extension.
specification: [MS-PPT] 2.1.2 permits unknown child records to be ignored and optionally preserved; 2.13.24 does not assign recType 0x0000.
upstream: Apache POI RecordTypes declares Unknown(0, null), preserves it through UnknownRecordPlaceholder, and TestBugs.bug38256 locks a real type-0 producer regression.

debt.compatibility.type_0x200a.summary: A producer-specific eight-byte handout atom has a stable layout but no assignment in the current MS-PPT RecordType enumeration.
specification: [MS-PPT] 2.1.2 defines preservation for records absent from 2.13.24; recType 0x200A is absent from that enumeration.
upstream: Neither Apache POI RecordTypes nor LibreOffice svdfppt.hxx assigns recType 0x200A; olecfsdk preserves its observed fixed eight-byte form as HandoutCompatibilityAtom.

debt.unknown_extension.type_0x0080.summary: Structurally framed zero-length record with an unassigned recType; its semantics remain unknown and its bytes are preserved.
specification: [MS-PPT] 2.1.2 requires unknown recType values to be ignored and permits preservation; 2.13.24 does not assign 0x0080.
upstream: Apache POI RecordTypes.forTypeID falls back to UnknownRecordPlaceholder, while LibreOffice svdfppt.hxx has no 0x0080 record constant.

debt.unknown_extension.type_0x779f.summary: Structurally framed four-byte record with an unassigned recType; its semantics remain unknown and its bytes are preserved.
specification: [MS-PPT] 2.1.2 requires unknown recType values to be ignored and permits preservation; 2.13.24 does not assign 0x779F.
upstream: Apache POI RecordTypes.forTypeID falls back to UnknownRecordPlaceholder, while LibreOffice svdfppt.hxx has no 0x779F record constant.

debt.malformed.type_0xf004.summary: recType 0xF004 is the known OfficeArtSpContainer container type, but one producer emitted it with atom framing; preserve the nonconforming body without calling it an extension.
specification: [MS-ODRAW] 2.2.14 defines OfficeArtSpContainer as a container record with recVer 0xF.
upstream: Apache POI EscherRecordTypes and LibreOffice OfficeArt constants both identify 0xF004 as the shape-container type.
corpus_examples: Audited DOC/XLS/PPT OfficeArt corpus (one atom-framed 0xF004 record, 66 body bytes)
